name: Russian Pastries
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, mix together the cream cheese, flour, and butter with your hands until smooth.
2. Shape the dough into a ball, wrap it in plastic wrap or foil, and refrigerate until chilled for about 1 hour.
3. In a 10-inch skillet over high heat, cook the ground beef and onion until the meat is browned and all pan juices have evaporated. Remove the skillet from the heat.
4. Stir in sour cream, pickle relish, salt, dill weed, pepper, chopped egg, and 1/4 cup water. Set aside.
5. On a floured surface with a floured rolling pin, roll the dough to about 1/8-inch thickness.
6. Using a floured 2-3/4-inch round cookie cutter, cut out dough rounds. Repeat with the remaining dough and trimmings to make about 50 dough rounds in total.
7. Place one teaspoon of meat mixture onto half of each round. In a small bowl, beat the remaining egg.
8. Brush the edges of the dough round with some beaten egg, fold the dough over the filling, and press the edges together firmly to seal. Prick the tops with a fork and brush the remaining egg over the pastries.
9. Arrange the pastries on an ungreased cookie sheet about 1-inch apart. If not serving immediately, cover with foil and refrigerate.
10. To serve, preheat the oven to 425°F. Bake the pastries for 10 minutes or until golden. Makes about 50 pastries.
Each pastry contains approximately 65 calories, 5 grams of fat, 18 milligrams of cholesterol, and 70 milligrams of sodium.
TO FREEZE AND USE UP TO 1 MONTH LATER:
Place the unbaked pastries in a freezer container with waxed paper between each layer. Seal, label, and freeze.
About 25 minutes before serving, preheat the oven to 425°F. Arrange the frozen pastries on an ungreased cookie sheet and bake for 15 minutes or until the pastries are golden.
